person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Dunstanville en latin, Rainaldus de Dunstanivilla
“Royal Ancestry: A Study in Colonial & Medieval Families,” Douglas Richardson (2013): 
  “ROBERT [II], Count of Meulan, son and heir of Waleran (or Galeran) [II], Count of Meulan, Earl of Worcester, seigneur of Beaumont-le-Roger, Brionne, la Croix Saint-Leufroy, Elbeuf, Port-Audemer, etc., by Agnes, daughter of Amaury de Montfort, Count of Evreux. He married about 1165 MAUD OF CORNWALL, daughter of Reynold Fitz Roy, Earl of Cornwall (illegitimate son of King Henry I), by Mabel, daughter of William Fitz Richard [see ENGLAND 2.i for her ancestry]. They had three sons, Waleran (or Galeran), Peter [Dean of Wimborne], and Henry, and two daughters, Mabel and Jeanne (wife of Guy IV, seigneur of La Roche Guyon). In 1173 he adhered to the young King Henry in his revolt against Henry II, but was reconciled to the king. Holding lands in both France and Normandy, the conflict between Philip Augustus and the English kings placed him in a difficult position, and eventually brought about his ruin. In 1188 when Philip took Vedôme, he captured 62 knights sent by the Count of Meulan to assist the defence. In 1191 he was excommunicated for taking action against William de Longchamp, Bishop of Ely. In 1202 he confirmed the charter of his sister, Isabel de Mayenne, to the monks of Savigny. In 1202 he revolted against King Philippe Auguste of France, and suffered the confiscation of his county of Meulan in consequence. Conversely, he had incurred King John's malevolentia before 2 April 1203, when the abbot of Saint-Taurin d'Evreux acted as an intermediary to reconcile him with the king of England. In a vain attempt to save his position, he handed over all of his estates 1 May 1204 to  his daughter, Mabel, and her husband, William de Vernon, Earl of Devon. John, however, seized his English estates, and Philippe those in France and Normandy. ROBERT, deposed Count of Meulan, was living 14 November 1207; he died 16 August, about 1212. His widow, Maud, was living 25 October 1212; she reportedly died 1220-1.
****************************

Foundation for Medieval Genealogy
    
MATILDA of Cornwall (-[1216/12 Feb 1221]).  Robert of Torigny records the marriage of "filius eius [Gualeranni comitis Mellenti] Robertus" and "filiam Rainaldi comitis Cornubiensis" but does not name her[208].  Letters dated 25 Oct 1212 record that King John ordered a pension payable to “Matildis comtesse de Meulan”[209].  Stapleton says that Matilda "survived through the whole reign of King John and held, of the inheritance of her father...two manors in [Cornwall]...St. Clement’s, otherwise Moris, and Rillaton in the parish of Stoke Climsland", which after her death were granted by King Henry III to "his favourite Falcasius de Breauté" 12 Feb 1221[210].  m ([1165]) ROBERT [II] Comte de Meulan, son of WALERAN de Beaumont[-le-Roger] Comte de Meulan [previously Earl of Worcester] & his wife Agnes de Montfort ([1142/43?]-[16 Aug or 20 Sep] 1208, bur Préaux).

zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud de Dunstanville於1143年出生在英國可瓦爾勞斯頓時，她的父親Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall44歲，母親Beatrice FitzWilliam28歲。她於大約1160年與Robert de Beaumont在英國戴文結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1207年10月1日在英國爾享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Maud de Dunstanville于1143年出生在英国可瓦爾勞斯頓时，她的父亲Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall44岁，母亲Beatrice FitzWilliam28岁。她于大约1160年与Robert de Beaumont在英国戴文结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1207年10月1日在英国爾享年64岁。

meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud de Dunstanville was born in 1143, in Launceston, Cornwall, England, her father, Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall, was 44 and her mother, Beatrice FitzWilliam, was 28. She married Robert de Beaumont about 1160, in Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 1 October 1207, in Cheshire, England, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud de Dunstanville est née en 1143, à Launceston, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall, avait 44 ans et sa mère, Beatrice FitzWilliam, avait 28 ans. Elle a épousé Robert de Beaumont environ 1160, à Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 octobre 1207, à Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud de Dunstanville 1143 in Launceston, Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall 44 und ihre Mutter Beatrice FitzWilliam 28. Sie hat um 1160 in Devon, England Robert de Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Oktober 1207 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Cheshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Dunstanville è nata il nel 1143 nel Launceston, Cornwall, England, sua padre, Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall, aveva 44 anni e sua madre, Beatrice FitzWilliam, aveva 28 anni. Lei sposato Robert de Beaumont circa 1160, nel Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 1 ottobre 1207, nel Cheshire, England, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud de Dunstanvilleが1143年にイギリスコルンワルラウンセストンで生まれた時，父Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwallは44歳，母Beatrice FitzWilliamは28歳であった。約1160年にイギリスデボンでRobert de Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1207年10月1日にイギリスチェシュアイアで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud de Dunstanville(이)가 1143년 영국 코른월 로스스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall(은)는 44세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Beatrice FitzWilliam(은)는 28세였다. 그녀는 대략 1160년 영국 데폰에서 Robert de Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1207년 10월 1일 영국 체실르에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Maud de Dunstanville nasceu em 1143, em Launceston, Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall, tinha 44 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice FitzWilliam, tinha 28 anos. Ela casou-se com Robert de Beaumont aproximadamente 1160, em Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de outubro de 1207, em Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud de Dunstanville родилась в 1143 г. в местности лаунсестон, корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall было 44 года, а матери, которую звали Beatrice FitzWilliam, 28. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert de Beaumont около 1160 г. в местности девон, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 1 октября 1207 г. в местности чешир, Англия в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Maud de Dunstanville nació en 1143, en Launceston, Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Reginald de Dunstanville Earl of Cornwall, tenía 44 años y su mamá, Beatrice FitzWilliam, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Robert de Beaumont aproximadamente 1160, en Devon, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 1 de octubre de 1207, en Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 64 años.
